title: Canon Registry Primitive v1
description: Schema candidate for P27 CanonRegistry — governs promotion from draft to canonical status. Closes the governance feedback loop: admission -> authority -> execution -> receipt -> evidence -> PROMOTION. Enforces D5 (NO_AUTO_PROMOTION): no agent can promote an artifact to canonical status without operator approval. Status: PROPOSED — not yet implemented.

artifact_id.description: Unique identifier of the artifact being promoted.

current_canon_level.description: Current canonical level of the artifact.

proposed_canon_level.description: Proposed next canonical level. Transitions must be sequential: draft -> reviewed -> validated -> adopted -> canonical. Deprecation can occur from any level.

operator_approval.description: Must be true for any promotion. Enforces D5 (NO_AUTO_PROMOTION).

approver_id.description: Identity of the operator approving the promotion.

delegation_chain.description: If approval was delegated, the chain of delegation token hashes.

authority.description: Authority gate: operator must explicitly approve promotion. Agents cannot self-promote.

evidence_refs.description: References to evidence supporting promotion (test results, peer review, validation receipts).

evidence.description: Evidence gate: what evidence supports this promotion.

receipt.description: Receipt gate: hash-chained proof of promotion.

reviewer_ids.description: Identities of reviewers who assessed the artifact.

review_conditions.description: Conditions that must be met for conditional_pass verdicts.

review.description: Review gate: peer review results (required for reviewed -> validated transition).

parent_artifact_id.description: If this artifact supersedes another, the parent's ID.

evolution_lineage_ref.description: Reference to evolution_lineage record tracking this artifact's ancestry.

lineage.description: Lineage tracking: connects to evolution_lineage primitive.
